summary: Audience recording salvages a solid 1994 Rosemont show, delivering clearer drums and mix despite the venue’s poor soundboard acoustics.
review: The reviewer rates the concert highly, calling the Rosemont run one of the best of 1994 and praising the performance overall. While the sound‑board mix is described as flat and brash with almost no drums, the front‑of‑house audience tape captures the drum work and overall balance much better, albeit with some crowd noise.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments about the show (4‑star rating) offset by criticism of the soundboard; the audience recording is deemed good, leading to an overall rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard mix described as flat, brash and lacking drums due to poor venue acoustics. The front‑of‑house audience recording captures drums and overall balance much better, with some crowd noise but all music present.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid, typical 1994 Rosemont performance that the reviewer found “tops by 1994 standards.” No specific set‑list issues were mentioned.
